findings.evidence: The endpoint fetch_remote_page accepts a user-controlled URL (payload.url) and directly uses it in requests.get without any validation or allowlisting.
findings.recommendation: Implement strict validation or an allowlist of permitted domains/URLs before making outbound requests to prevent Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) attacks.

summary: The code allows an attacker to supply arbitrary URLs to the /fetch endpoint, which are then fetched by the server without validation. This creates a high risk of SSRF attacks. No other security issues were identified.
